pr.title: lb: improve fn_80027488 matching, tidy a few header signatures
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Improved fuzzy matching for lb audio function fn_80027488 by replacing synthetic struct-field accesses through lbl_80433710 with direct references to the standalone extern arrays lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984, whose addresses correspond to those former offsets. The PR also cleaned several lb header declarations by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders with explicit void signatures already used by the corresponding C implementations. Build verification passed, but fn_80027488 was still not a full match.

details: In fn_80027488, changed int* a from lbl_80433710.x194 to direct extern lbl_804338A4.
Changed int* b from lbl_80433710.x274 to direct extern lbl_80433984.
Declared b before a so MWCC materializes addresses in an order closer to the original code.
Changed lb_8000FA94 from UNK_RET lb_8000FA94(UNK_PARAMS) to void lb_8000FA94(void).
Changed lb_80014534 from UNK_RET lb_80014534(UNK_PARAMS) to void lb_80014534(void).
Changed lb_8001955C, fn_800195FC, lb_800198E0, and lb_80019900 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to explicit void(void) declarations.
Changed lb_8001C600, lb_8001CF18, and lb_8001D1F4 to explicit void(void) declarations.
Changed lb_8001C658 from const char* lb_8001C658(UNK_PARAMS) to const char* lb_8001C658(void).

smart_moves: Used symbol-address knowledge from symbols.txt to model lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984 as standalone arrays rather than as fields inside lbl_80433710, reducing artificial struct coupling and improving codegen.
Reordered local declarations in fn_80027488, declaring b before a, to influence MWCC address materialization order toward the original binary.

Kept header cleanups ABI-neutral: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with void did not change compilation semantics because the macros already expanded to void, but it made the declarations more truthful and searchable.
decomp_lessons: When a symbol appears as an offset into a larger guessed struct, check whether symbols.txt also names the exact address as a standalone object; direct externs can produce closer matching code.
Local variable declaration order can affect MWCC register/address setup order, especially around multiple global-address loads.
Header declarations should be tightened from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to explicit signatures when matching C definitions already prove the ABI.
A decomp improvement can be worth landing even without a 100% function match if it materially improves fuzzy similarity and does not regress nearby functions.
naming_conventions: Standalone global labels are kept in lbl_XXXXXXXX form when semantic names are not yet known, e.g. lbl_804338A4 and lbl_80433984.
Library functions in lb headers continue using address-derived names such as lb_8001955C, while unidentified non-library-local functions may use fn_XXXXXXXX, such as fn_800195FC and fn_80027488.
Use explicit void parameter lists for no-argument C prototypes instead of UNK_PARAMS once confirmed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replacing struct-field global accesses with direct extern array references improved fn_80027488 fuzzy match from 89.55% to 94.86%.
Declaration order was deliberately used to affect MWCC emitted address-load order.
Remaining mismatch was reported as an extra prologue/register-cascade difference: MWCC emitted lis r3/lis r3 for two HA halves while the original used lis r4/lis r3.
Verification included python configure.py && ninja with build/GALE01/main.dol OK and no reported lbaudio_ax.c regressions.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR summary explicitly documented that fn_80027488 was not a full 100% match and identified the remaining compiler-codegen mismatch.
follow_up_queries: Search for other lbl_80433710 field accesses that correspond to standalone symbols and may match better as extern arrays.
Audit lb headers for remaining UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS declarations where the C implementation already proves void(void) or another concrete ABI.
Investigate whether fn_80027488 can eliminate the remaining MWCC prologue/register-cascade mismatch involving lis r3/lis r3 versus lis r4/lis r3.
